resposta:
solution: ```class Solution:
    def maxAbsValExpr(self, arr1: List[int], arr2: List[int]) -> int:
        n = len(arr1)
        max1 = max2 = max3 = max4 = float('-inf')
        min1 = min2 = min3 = min4 = float('inf')
        for i in range(n):
            v1 = arr1[i] + arr2[i] + i
            v2 = arr1[i] + arr2[i] - i
            v3 = arr1[i] - arr2[i] + i
            v4 = arr1[i] - arr2[i] - i
            max1, min1 = max(max1, v1), min(min1, v1)
            max2, min2 = max(max2, v2), min(min2, v2)
            max3, min3 = max(max3, v3), min(min3, v3)
            max4, min4 = max(max4, v4), min(min4, v4)
        return max(max1 - min1, max2 - min2, max3 - min3, max4 - min4)
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The expression is rewritten as max over four linear forms; by tracking the max and min of each form in one pass we obtain the result without nested loops.
code:
class Solution:
    def maxAbsValExpr(self, arr1: List[int], arr2: List[int]) -> int:
        n = len(arr1)
        max1 = max2 = max3 = max4 = float('-inf')
        min1 = min2 = min3 = min4 = float('inf')
        for i in range(n):
            v1 = arr1[i] + arr2[i] + i
            v2 = arr1[i] + arr2[i] - i
            v3 = arr1[i] - arr2[i] + i
            v4 = arr1[i] - arr2[i] - i
            max1, min1 = max(max1, v1), min(min1, v1)
            max2, min2 = max(max2, v2), min(min2, v2)
            max3, min3 = max(max3, v3), min(min3, v3)
            max4, min4 = max(max4, v4), min(min4, v4)
        return max(max1 - min1, max2 - min2, max3 - min3, max4 - min4)
